'''Cob-caree''' was a wealthy [[Saffa (species)|Saffa]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who embarked upon a [[Morodin/Legends|Morodin]]-hunting expedition on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Varonat/Legends|Varonat]] in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]. Joining an expedition led by the [[Krish (species)/Legends|Krish]], [[Falmal]], Cob-caree was successful in [[Death/Legends|killing]] a Morodin alongside the [[Thennqora]], [[Tamish]].

==Biography==
Cob-caree was a wealthy [[Saffa (species)|Saffa]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. He traveled to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Varonat/Legends|Varonat]] on the [[Ison Trade Corridor/Legends|Ison Corridor]]<ref name="FC" /> in the [[Anoat sector/Legends|Anoat sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA-OC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> In the main settlement of [[Tropis-on-Varonat]], Cob-caree joined a [[Morodin/Legends|Morodin]]-hunting safari run by the [[Krish (species)/Legends|Krish]], [[Gamgalon]]. Alongside five others—a [[Thennqora]] named [[Tamish]], two [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] called [[Hav]] and [[Jivis]], and the [[Human/Legends|Humans]] [[Talon Karrde|Pandic Hart]] and [[Quelev Tapper|Seoul]]—Cob-caree planned to kill a Morodin, which were considered to be easy game for a beginner hunter. He purchased hunting gear from [[Great Jungle Outfitters]] like all those who embarked upon the safaris.<ref name="FC" />

Cob-caree was paired with Tamish in an [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]], along with a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] guide, [[Jombo]], for travel to the safari base camp. The group then hiked for several [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] through the jungle to reach the hunting trail to make camp. Cob-caree had trouble setting up his own tent, and required the aid of the guides to pitch it properly. During the task, the distinctive growl of a morodin was heard from the jungle close by. [[Falmal]], the Krish party leader, instructed his charges to split into the groups of three that had traveled together on the airspeeders. Cob-caree and Tamish, with Jombo, headed into the jungle in search of the Morodin. Following a slime trail left by the Morodin, Cob-caree and his companions came upon the creature and opened fire, [[Death/Legends|killing]] it.<ref name="FC" />

Falmal congratulated Cob-caree and Tamish on the kill, and arranged for the airspeeders to return to prepare their trophies. For the next two hours, the meat from the Morodin was [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttled]] in from the kill site, and the airpeeder [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] held lengthy conversations regarding which one got part of the head as a trophy, and their preferences in terms of mounting and framing their trophy.<ref name="FC" />

==Personality and traits==
Cob-caree was a wealthy Saffa who was bored and looking for excitement, leading to him signing up for a hunting expedition. Despite his wealthy, Cob-caree complained that he had to work for his excitement. He was trained in the use of [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] weapons, and managed to kill a Morodin during the hunt.<ref name="FC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Cob-caree first appeared in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s ''[[First Contact]]'', which was published in [[1994]]'s [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 1]] by [[West End Games]]. ''First Contact'' was subsequently reprinted in ''[[The Best of the Star Wars Adventure Journal, Issues 1-4]]'', ''[[Tales from the Empire]]'', and on [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|''Hyperspace'']].
